chapter_context.summary: Depicts Belshazzar's blasphemous feast using the golden vessels plundered from Jerusalem's Temple, the sudden appearance of a supernatural hand writing on the plaster wall, and Daniel decoding the message (Mene, Mene, Tekel, Upharsin) as the immediate fall of Babylon to the Medes and Persians.

outline.text: Belshazzar's Sacrilegious Feast and the Writing Hand on the Wall
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Belshazzar's Sacrilegious Feast and the Writing Hand on the Wall (5:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: The Queen's Recommendation and the Summoning of Daniel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Queen's Recommendation and the Summoning of Daniel (5:10-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Daniel's Rebuke of Belshazzar's Pride and Warning of Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel's Rebuke of Belshazzar's Pride and Warning of Judgment (5:17-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Decoding: Mene, Tekel, Upharsin — Numbered, Weighed, and Divided
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Decoding: Mene, Tekel, Upharsin — Numbered, Weighed, and Divided (5:25-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Babylon's Fall: Belshazzar Slain and Darius the Mede Accession
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Babylon's Fall: Belshazzar Slain and Darius the Mede Accession (5:30-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 5, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
